summary: Decompiled sysdolphin/baselib util.s into util.c and added a matching util.h, moving the object list from the asm object to the C object. The new C file covers HSD_MulColor, a 32-bit set-bit counting routine left with the placeholder name func_8037A7EC and a comment suggesting HSD_CountSetBits, a switch-based function func_8037A8AC that returns multiples of 3 for cases 0 through 9 and asserts otherwise, plus the float data symbol lbl_80406DF0. No PR body, comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 201-line assembly implementation at asm/sysdolphin/baselib/util.s.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/util.c with lbl_80406DF0 data and C implementations for HSD_MulColor, func_8037A7EC, and func_8037A8AC.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/util.h with include guards, includes, function prototypes, and an extern declaration for lbl_80406DF0.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/util.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/util.s.o.
smart_moves: Kept uncertain symbols as address-derived placeholders instead of over-naming: func_8037A7EC and func_8037A8AC remain unnamed, with only a tentative comment for HSD_CountSetBits.
Modeled HSD_MulColor directly with GXColor channel fields, matching the repeated byte loads/stores from the assembly and making the intent clearer.
Represented the former jump-table behavior in func_8037A8AC as a switch over cases 0 through 9, preserving the assert path for out-of-range inputs.
Preserved the existing data symbol lbl_80406DF0 as an exported float array rather than inventing a semantic name not evidenced by the PR.
Completed the decompilation handoff by changing obj_files.mk from the asm object to the C object.
decomp_lessons: For small baselib utility files, a complete asm-to-C replacement can include code, data symbols, header declarations, and object-list migration in one PR.
When a function's behavior is clear but its official name is not, retain the func_address name and optionally document the hypothesis in a comment.
A PowerPC jump table returning a simple sequence can often match cleanly as a C switch with explicit case returns, while retaining the default __assert call.
Byte-wise color multiplication by 255 can be expressed idiomatically as per-channel GXColor assignments using unsigned division by 255U.
Data formerly emitted in asm .data can be moved to C as a global array when layout and values are simple and known.
naming_conventions: Public HSD symbol retained with its existing name: HSD_MulColor.
Unknown functions kept in address-based form: func_8037A7EC and func_8037A8AC.
Unknown data symbol kept in address-based lbl form: lbl_80406DF0.
The source comment suggests, but does not confirm, the name HSD_CountSetBits for func_8037A7EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly color multiply sequence using mulhwu with a high-level expression `(channel0 * channel1) / 255U` for each GXColor channel.
Translated the unrolled 32-bit bit-counting loop into `for (i = 0; i < 32; i++)` with `(c & (1 << i))` and a counter increment.
Translated the jump table jtbl_80406E20 into a switch statement with returns 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, and 27.
Preserved the default assert from assembly as `__assert("util.c", 132, "0")`, matching the original file-name and line-number evidence.
Moved the object list from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/util.s.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/util.c.o to ensure the C translation is built.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for func_8037A7EC or HSD_CountSetBits to see whether the tentative name was confirmed.
Search later PRs for func_8037A8AC to identify its semantic purpose and callers.
Search for lbl_80406DF0 usage to determine whether the float array represents a matrix or another baselib constant.
Check whether util.h includes such as objalloc.h and math.h were later trimmed or replaced.
